tomato_late_blight.chemical: Apply copper-based fungicide or chlorothalonil every 7-10 days
tomato_late_blight.organic: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Apply neem oil as preventive
preventive: Use certified disease-free seeds and transplants
Space plants for good air circulation
Avoid overhead watering - use drip irrigation
Apply mulch to prevent soil splash
Remove lower leaves that touch the ground
Rotate crops - don't plant tomatoes in same spot for 3 years

tomato_early_blight.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicide every 7-14 days
tomato_early_blight.organic: Use copper spray or baking soda solution (1 tbsp per gallon water)
preventive: Apply thick organic mulch around plants
Water at soil level, keep foliage dry
Remove lower leaves as plant grows
Maintain adequate plant nutrition with balanced fertilizer
Stake plants for better air circulation
Monitor plants weekly for early disease signs
Prune suckers for better air flow
Keep garden area clean of debris

tomato_bacterial_spot.chemical: Apply copper-based bactericide at first sign
tomato_bacterial_spot.organic: No effective organic cure - focus on prevention and removal of infected plants
preventive: Don't work with plants when wet

tomato_leaf_mold.organic: Improve ventilation, reduce humidity, apply sulfur-based fungicides
preventive: Ensure excellent air circulation in greenhouses

potato_late_blight.organic: Destroy infected plants, use copper spray preventively
preventive: Hill soil around plants to protect tubers

potato_early_blight.chemical: Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil fungicide every 7-14 days
potato_early_blight.organic: Use copper spray or sulfur-based fungicide

corn_common_rust.organic: Usually not economical to treat - use resistant varieties

grape_black_rot.chemical: Apply myclobutanil or mancozeb from bloom to 6 weeks after
grape_black_rot.organic: Remove mummified fruit, apply copper or sulfur fungicides
preventive: Remove all mummies from vines and ground
Apply fungicide starting at bud break
Train vines to keep fruit off ground

apple_scab.chemical: Apply captan or myclobutanil starting at bud break
apple_scab.organic: Apply sulfur or copper spray, rake and destroy fallen leaves
preventive: Rake and destroy fallen leaves in autumn
Apply dormant oil spray in early spring
